company.name: Praxis Precision Medicines
company.subtitle: 2 NDAs filed (ulixacaltamide ET + relutrigine DEEs) · PDUFA Sep 2026 · Best-in-class CNS pipeline

market.notes: ~7M US patients with ET, most undertreated. No new pharmacological treatment in decades (only propranolol/primidone, both off-label). Essential3 Phase 3 positive. NDA filed early 2026. PDUFA Sep 27, 2026. BTD granted. Oral, selective T-type calcium channel inhibitor — novel mechanism. Pre-launch activities underway.

us.patientsK.note: International Essential Tremor Foundation/NINDS: ~500K US on treatment (propranolol/primidone base); ~7M US total prevalence.

eu.patientsK.note: EU ET societies: ~1M treated ET EU27+UK; ~14M total prevalence.

row.patientsK.note: Global prevalence ~1% age 40+; ~60M worldwide, <10% treated in LMICs.

us.wtpPct.note: ~60% - Medicare Part D covers propranolol/primidone generics; DBS reserved for severe refractory; Jazz Suvecaltamide pipeline.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~45% - EU generic beta-blockers first-line; DBS via NICE/G-BA for refractory.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% - Japan covered; emerging markets limited to propranolol generics.

us.priceK.note: Blended $1.5K: propranolol/primidone generics $0.3K (85% of pts), DBS $75K one-time amortized, pipeline CaV3.x inhibitors $20K+.

penPct.note: ~25% peak - Suvecaltamide (Jazz) and other T-type Ca channel blockers expanding branded share if approved.

us.reachPct.note: NDA filed with BTD for ET -- first new pharmacological treatment in decades for 500K diagnosed US patients; 12% reach post-approval

us.priceK.note: Specialty oral CNS pricing ~$15K/yr; novel mechanism but ET market has no pricing precedent for new Rx

market.notes: Ultra-rare severe pediatric epilepsies. EMBOLD registrational data strong. NDA filed early 2026. BTD + Orphan. First precision therapy for SCN2A/SCN8A DEEs. High-pricing orphan ($200-400K/yr).

us.patientsK.note: Epilepsy Foundation/NINDS: ~30K US developmental and epileptic encephalopathies (LGS, Dravet, SCN2A, CDKL5).

eu.patientsK.note: Orphanet/ILAE EU: ~35K prevalent DEE across EU27+UK; LGS ~40% of treated, Dravet ~20%.

row.patientsK.note: WHO 2023: ~150K global DEE ex-US/EU; Japan PMDA covers Epidiolex and Fintepla for Dravet/LGS.

us.wtpPct.note: ~75% - Medicaid and commercial cover Epidiolex/Fintepla/Ztalmy for LGS/Dravet/CDKL5 with PA; pediatric neuro centers drive uptake.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~55% - EMA approved Epidiolex and Fintepla; NICE/G-BA reimburse with genetic confirmation requirements.

row.wtpPct.note: ~20% - Japan PMDA covered; emerging markets limited to valproate/clobazam generics.

us.priceK.note: Blended $45K: Epidiolex $32K, Fintepla $96K, Ztalmy $100K, ASO pipeline $300K+; older AEDs (clobazam/valproate) $3K.

eu.priceK.note: ~$25K blended; EU net ~55% of US list; generic AED backbone with branded add-on.

row.priceK.note: ~$8K blended; Japan near EU, rest generics only.

penPct.note: ~40% peak - genetic diagnosis rate rising with NGS panels; ASO/gene therapy pipeline expanding addressable.

us.reachPct.note: NDA filed BTD+orphan for ultra-rare pediatric DEEs; 20% reach in small 30K US pop reflects high unmet need + precision targeting

us.priceK.note: Orphan rare epilepsy pricing ~$250K/yr; SCN2A/SCN8A precision therapy commands orphan premium (Epidiolex $40K, Ztalmy $70K -- but this is more targeted)

market.notes: ~3.5M US epilepsy patients. Most potent NaV modulator ever developed. RADIANT Phase 2 presented at AES 2025 — best-in-disease profile. Phase 3 ongoing. Once-daily oral. Competitors: lacosamide, brivaracetam, cenobamate.

us.patientsK.note: NIH/CDC: ~1.2M US treated focal (partial-onset) epilepsy; ~60% of ~3.4M total epilepsy prevalence.

eu.patientsK.note: ILAE EU: ~2.5M treated focal epilepsy EU27+UK; ~6M total epilepsy prevalence.

row.patientsK.note: WHO 2023: ~30M global epilepsy; ~18M focal; 80% treatment gap in LMICs.

us.wtpPct.note: ~85% - Medicare Part D and commercial cover Vimpat/Briviact/Xcopri; 30% refractory drive branded AED use.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~70% - EMA approved all modern AEDs; generics dominate first-line, branded for refractory.

row.wtpPct.note: ~35% - Japan broad coverage; emerging markets heavy generic (levetiracetam/carbamazepine).

us.priceK.note: Blended $4K: levetiracetam generic $0.5K (70% of pts), Xcopri $12K, Briviact $14K, Vimpat generic $2K.

eu.priceK.note: ~$2K blended; EU net ~50% of US, generic levetiracetam backbone.

penPct.note: ~70% peak - large treated pool but branded share limited by generic levetiracetam first-line.

us.reachPct.note: Ph3 best-in-disease NaV modulator; 6% reach in 1.2M epilepsy patients reflects crowded ASM market (lacosamide, cenobamate)

market.notes: 77% placebo-adjusted seizure reduction (p=0.015) in Phase 2. 71% achieved >50% seizure reduction. Solidus ASO platform (collaboration with Ionis). FDA aligned on simplified registrational path. EMBRAVE3 Phase 3 ongoing.

us.patientsK.note: Epilepsy Foundation/NINDS: ~30K US developmental and epileptic encephalopathies (LGS, Dravet, SCN2A, CDKL5).

eu.patientsK.note: Orphanet/ILAE EU: ~35K prevalent DEE across EU27+UK; LGS ~40% of treated, Dravet ~20%.

row.patientsK.note: WHO 2023: ~150K global DEE ex-US/EU; Japan PMDA covers Epidiolex and Fintepla for Dravet/LGS.

us.wtpPct.note: ~75% - Medicaid and commercial cover Epidiolex/Fintepla/Ztalmy for LGS/Dravet/CDKL5 with PA; pediatric neuro centers drive uptake.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~55% - EMA approved Epidiolex and Fintepla; NICE/G-BA reimburse with genetic confirmation requirements.

row.wtpPct.note: ~20% - Japan PMDA covered; emerging markets limited to valproate/clobazam generics.

us.priceK.note: Blended $45K: Epidiolex $32K, Fintepla $96K, Ztalmy $100K, ASO pipeline $300K+; older AEDs (clobazam/valproate) $3K.

eu.priceK.note: ~$25K blended; EU net ~55% of US list; generic AED backbone with branded add-on.

row.priceK.note: ~$8K blended; Japan near EU, rest generics only.

penPct.note: ~40% peak - genetic diagnosis rate rising with NGS panels; ASO/gene therapy pipeline expanding addressable.

us.reachPct.note: Ph3 ASO for rare SCN2A-DEE; 18% reach in 30K US patients reflects strong Ph2 data (77% seizure reduction)

us.priceK.note: Orphan ASO for rare epilepsy ~$250K/yr (nusinersen/Spinraza class pricing for rare CNS ASO)

market.notes: Expanding Solidus and Cerebrum platforms across rare genetic epilepsies. Multiple preclinical programs targeting distinct genetic drivers. Each is an orphan opportunity.

us.patientsK.note: NIH/CDC: ~400K US treated generalized epilepsy (JME, absence, tonic-clonic); ~15% of total epilepsy.

eu.patientsK.note: ILAE EU: ~900K treated generalized epilepsy EU27+UK.

row.patientsK.note: WHO 2023: ~9M global generalized epilepsy; valproate restrictions (women of childbearing age) drive switching.

us.wtpPct.note: ~85% - Medicare Part D and commercial broad AED coverage; valproate/lamotrigine/levetiracetam generics first-line.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~70% - EMA valproate restrictions 2018 shifted generalized epilepsy to lamotrigine/levetiracetam.

row.wtpPct.note: ~35% - Japan broad; emerging markets valproate-heavy despite teratogenicity concerns.

us.priceK.note: Blended $3K: valproate/lamotrigine/levetiracetam generics $0.5K (80% of pts), Briviact $14K, newer AEDs $10K+.

penPct.note: ~65% peak - generics dominate; branded penetration limited to refractory/teratogenicity-driven switches.

us.reachPct.note: Preclinical rare genetic epilepsy programs (KCNT1, PCDH19, SYNGAP1); 1% reach in 400K reflects very early stage

us.priceK.note: Rare genetic epilepsy pricing ~$80K/yr; mix of small molecule and ASO programs

mega_bear.narrative: FDA rejects both NDAs — safety or efficacy concerns derail ET + DEE launches
events: ✗ Ulixacaltamide CRL — FDA requests additional safety data
✗ Relutrigine CRL — single-arm EMBOLD data insufficient
✗ Vormatrigine Phase 3 fails to differentiate from SOC in FOS
→ $8B market cap collapses to $1.5B cash floor (~$55/share)

bear.narrative: ET approved but slow uptake + DEE narrow label + vormatrigine crowded
events: ✓ Ulixacaltamide approved but physicians slow to adopt new mechanism
~ Relutrigine approved but very narrow DEE population limits revenue
✗ Vormatrigine Phase 3 positive but incremental vs cenobamate
→ Revenue ramp disappoints, market questions $8B valuation

base.narrative: Dual launch ET + DEE + vormatrigine best-in-class potential confirmed
events: ✓ Ulixacaltamide approved Sep 2026 — strong launch in underserved ET market
✓ Relutrigine approved — first precision therapy for DEEs
✓ Vormatrigine Phase 3 best-in-class data — NDA track
~ Elsunersen Phase 3 on track — platform expanding

bull.narrative: CNS franchise leader — ET blockbuster + DEE dominant + epilepsy platform
events: ✓ Ulixacaltamide blockbuster — $2B+ peak in ET (massive unmet need)
✓ Relutrigine expands to broader DEEs beyond SCN2A/8A
✓ Vormatrigine best-in-class epilepsy drug — replaces lacosamide/cenobamate
✓ Elsunersen + preclinical programs build rare epilepsy franchise

psychedelic_bull.narrative: Precision neuroscience platform — 5+ products, $10B+ revenue — J&J/Roche acquires at $600+
events: ✓ Ulixacaltamide $3B+ peak — redefines ET treatment globally
✓ Relutrigine + elsunersen dominate genetic epilepsies
✓ Vormatrigine replaces all first-gen NaV modulators in adult epilepsy
✓ Platform generates 5+ approved products — J&J or Roche acquires at $600+

lastData.title: Two NDAs filed + elsunersen Phase 2 positive
points: Relutrigine NDA filed — SCN2A/SCN8A DEEs (BTD + Orphan)
Vormatrigine RADIANT Phase 2: best-in-disease at AES 2025
Pre-launch activities underway for both ulixacaltamide and relutrigine
Hired Dr. Orrin Devinsky (global epilepsy leader) as Head of Clinical Strategy

nextCatalyst.title: Ulixacaltamide PDUFA — first new ET pharmacotherapy in decades
thresholds: If approved: immediate commercial launch (pre-launch underway)

risks: $8.2B market cap for pre-revenue company — high expectations baked in
ET market has no precedent for pharmacological blockbuster — adoption uncertain
Relutrigine NDA based on single-arm EMBOLD — regulatory risk higher than RCT
Vormatrigine faces cenobamate (SK Biosciences) which is already gaining share
Stock went from $30 to $310 in 12 months — 10x with zero revenue
Heavy cash burn (~$300M/yr) despite $1.55B war chest
CNS commercial execution is historically difficult for biotech
Short seller report challenged Essential3 data integrity

catalysts.title: Relutrigine SCN2A/SCN8A DEE PDUFA
catalysts._rationale: FDA assigned PDUFA target action date of September 27, 2026 for relutrigine NDA

catalysts.title: EMERALD trial broad DEEs completion
catalysts._rationale: EMERALD trial in broad DEEs expected to be completed by end of 2026
